message:
fix(runtime): Comprehensive float formatting with banker's rounding

Float formatting improvements:
- Centralize banker's rounding in conversion.zig, used by all float formatting
- Fix scientific notation: normalize mantissa first, then apply banker's rounding
- Fix %g alternate form (#): always include decimal point, don't strip zeros
- Route integer inputs to float path for 'e', 'f', 'g', '%' formats
- Fix precision 0 handling for g format (treated as precision 1 per Python)
- Fix trailing zero stripping to only apply after decimal point

Type system enhancements:
- Add UnifiedInt support for list literals and module-level assignments
- Improve type inference for callable returns and loop variables
- Enhanced BigInt comparison in equality.zig

Codegen fixes:
- Fix raise statements for error propagation in class methods
- Improve closure generation for nested functions
- Better handling of generators and for-loops with tuple unpacking

test_float: 195 tests, test_format now passes (was failing)
Remaining: test_format_testfile (dtoa precision), test_repr (file reading)

files.patch:
@@ -196,6 +196,7 @@ pub const NativeType = union(enum) {
     bytes: void, // runtime.builtins.PyBytes - Python bytes type (preserves type info for repr)
     complex: void, // runtime.PyComplex - complex number
     int_result: void, // runtime.IntResult - tagged union (i64 small, BigInt big) from float rounding
+    pow_result: void, // runtime.builtins.PyPowResult - tagged union (float_val or complex_val) from pow with float exponent
 
     // Composites
     array: struct {
@@ -367,6 +368,7 @@ pub const NativeType = union(enum) {
             .bigint => try buf.appendSlice(allocator, "runtime.BigInt"),
             .unified_int => try buf.appendSlice(allocator, "runtime.UnifiedInt"),
             .int_result => try buf.appendSlice(allocator, "runtime.IntResult"),
+            .pow_result => try buf.appendSlice(allocator, "runtime.builtins.PyPowResult"),
             .usize => try buf.appendSlice(allocator, "usize"),
             .float => try buf.appendSlice(allocator, "f64"),
             .bool => try buf.appendSlice(allocator, "bool"),
